topic: Anomalous Diffusion & Fractional Calculus: Fractional Calculus for Non-Gaussian Processes
lesson: Based on my review of the last experiment: 1. This cycle taught me that a Riesz fractional derivative with an optimal fractional order between 0.4 and 0.8 can accurately predict the mean squared displacement (MSD) of particles in a non-Gaussian process, specifically for anomalous diffusion exponents α > 1. 2. To improve my chances in the next hypothesis, I should incorporate the Mittag-Leffler function into my fractional calculus model to better capture the memory effects and non-locality present in non-Gaussian processes with α < 1, which would require a structural change to my current modeling approach.
